title: ficus laevigata var. lentiginosa
intro: Ficus laevigata var. lentiginosa, also known as the Port Jackson fig or Queensland fig, is a variety of fig tree native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. Its leaves and bark are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, with reported benefits including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and relief from digestive discomfort. Herbal preparations of Ficus laevigata var. lentiginosa include infusions, tinctures, and salves made from its leaves and bark, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
